title: Rotated Sorted Array with Duplicate Elements

statement.markdown: Implement a function `solve` that performs binary search on a rotated sorted array with duplicate elements. The array is rotated an unknown number of times and may contain duplicate elements. The function should return the index of the target element if it exists, or -1 if it does not.
statement.inputFormat: Input will be a list of integers and a target integer
statement.outputFormat: The index of the target element if found, or -1 if not found
statement.notes: Do not use a sort function.

examples.explanation: The target element 0 is found at index 4

starterCode.cpp: class Solution { public: int solve(vector<int>& nums) { // TODO: implement } };
starterCode.java: public class Solution { public int solve(int[] nums) { // TODO: implement } }
starterCode.python: class Solution: def solve(self, nums: list[int]) -> int: # TODO: implement pass

buggyStarterCode.cpp: class Solution { public: int solve(vector<int>& nums) { int left = 0, right = nums.size() - 1; while (left <= right) { int mid = left + (right - left) / 2; if (nums[mid] == target) return mid; if (nums[left] <= nums[mid]) { if (nums[left] <= target && target <= nums[mid]) right = mid - 1; else left = mid + 1; } else { if (nums[mid] <= target && target <= nums[right]) left = mid + 1; else right = mid - 1; } } return -1; } };
buggyStarterCode.java: public class Solution { public int solve(int[] nums) { int left = 0, right = nums.length - 1; while (left <= right) { int mid = left + (right - left) / 2; if (nums[mid] == target) return mid; if (nums[left] <= nums[mid]) { if (nums[left] <= target && target <= nums[mid]) right = mid - 1; else left = mid + 1; } else { if (nums[mid] <= target && target <= nums[right]) left = mid + 1; else right = mid - 1; } } return -1; } }

functionSignature.java: public boolean solve(int[] nums, int target);
functionSignature.python: def solve(self, nums: list[int], target: int) -> bool:

solutions.cpp: #include <iostream> #include <vector> using namespace std; class Solution { public: int solve(vector<int>& nums, int target) { int left = 0, right = nums.size() - 1; while (left <= right) { int mid = left + (right - left) / 2; if (nums[mid] == target) return mid; if (nums[left] <= nums[mid]) { if (nums[left] <= target && target <= nums[mid]) right = mid - 1; else left = mid + 1; } else { if (nums[mid] <= target && target <= nums[right]) left = mid + 1; else right = mid - 1; } } return -1; } }; int main() { vector<int> nums = {4, 5, 5, 6, 7, 0, 1, 2, 3}; int target = 0; Solution sol; cout << sol.solve(nums, target) << endl; return 0; }
solutions.python: import sys class Solution: def solve(self, nums: list[int], target: int) -> int: left = 0 right = len(nums) - 1 while left <= right: mid = left + (right - left) // 2 if nums[mid] == target: return mid if nums[left] <= nums[mid]: if nums[left] <= target and target <= nums[mid]: right = mid - 1 else: left = mid + 1 else: if nums[mid] <= target and target <= nums[right]: left = mid + 1 else: right = mid - 1 return -1 sol = Solution() nums = [4, 5, 5, 6, 7, 0, 1, 2, 3] target = 0 print(sol.solve(nums, target))
